Class BaseScreen

java.lang.Object
ui.BaseScreen
Direct Known Subclasses:
DashboardScreen, ExpenseLoggingScreen, HistoryScreen, LockScreen, SettingsScreen, SetupScreen
public abstract class BaseScreen extends Object
Abstract base class for all application screens.

Provides the shared JPanel root, a reference to the App facade, and the reusable left-side navigation bar. Subclasses implement initComponents() to build their widgets and initLayout() to arrange them.

    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    protected App
    app
    Application facade used to trigger navigation and business logic.
    protected JPanel
    panel
    Root panel returned to the JFrame content pane.

  • Field Details

    • panel

      protected JPanel panel
      Root panel returned to the JFrame content pane.

    • app

      protected App app
      Application facade used to trigger navigation and business logic.

  • Constructor Details

    • BaseScreen

      public BaseScreen(App app)
      Constructs the screen, stores the app reference, and calls initComponents() followed by initLayout().
      Parameters:
      app - the central application controller

  • Method Details

    • createNavBar

      public JPanel createNavBar(String activeItem)
      Builds and returns the shared vertical navigation bar. The active item is highlighted with a purple indicator strip.
      Parameters:
      activeItem - display name of the currently active nav item (e.g., "DashBoard", "History", "Settings")
      Returns:
      the fully constructed nav bar panel

    • createNavItem

      protected JPanel createNavItem(String text, boolean active, Runnable onClick)
      Creates a single navigation item consisting of an optional active indicator strip and a clickable button.
      Parameters:
      text - label shown on the nav button
      active - true if this item represents the current screen
      onClick - action to run when the item is clicked
      Returns:
      wrapper panel containing the indicator and button

    • createNavButton

      protected JButton createNavButton(String text, boolean active)
      Creates a styled navigation button with left-aligned text and no border.
      Parameters:
      text - button label
      active - true to apply the active background colour
      Returns:
      the configured JButton

    • initComponents

      protected abstract void initComponents()
      Initialises all UI components (fields, buttons, labels, etc.). Called before initLayout().

    • initLayout

      protected abstract void initLayout()
      Arranges the initialised components into the panel hierarchy. Called after initComponents().

    • getPanel

      public JPanel getPanel()
      Returns the root panel to be set as the JFrame content pane.
      Returns:
      the screen's root JPanel
